items.title: Restore AceData Skills Cloning for Maestro in Platform Service 🚀
items.summary: This update un-vendors 18 AceData provider skills from the maestro worker, ensuring a single source of truth for updates. The Dockerfile now clones the necessary skills and includes a new smoke check for link resolution, preventing dead links in documentation. Overall, this change enhances maintainability and resolves existing documentation issues.

items.title: Fix share menu icon registration in Nexior 🎨
items.summary: This update addresses the missing icon for the 'share' menu item in the chat sidebar by registering the `fa-share-nodes` icon in the centralized Font Awesome plugin. The fix involves adding two lines to the `src/plugins/font-awesome.ts` file to ensure the icon is properly imported and registered. This change enhances the visual consistency of the menu options.

items.title: Enhance WeCom with Media Sending Capabilities in Platform Service 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces the ability to send images, files, and videos through the WeCom service, addressing a major gap in functionality compared to Wisdom. Key changes include modifications to the visual backend for media attachment and updates to the API to handle new media types. Security measures have been implemented to prevent SSRF vulnerabilities, ensuring safe media handling.

items.title: Enhance Android Skills Installation in Nexior 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces a new section in the Local Tools settings specifically for Android users, allowing them to install the `xhs-dm` skill with a single tap. This feature enhances user experience by integrating skill installation directly within the Computer Use settings, making it more accessible for Android users. Additionally, the update includes internationalization support for multiple languages.

items.title: Enhance Audio Sync and Visual Quality in Platform Service 🎨
items.summary: This update addresses key quality issues identified during local renders, including audio desynchronization and visual clarity. The timeline for captions is now accurately pinned to audio durations, ensuring scenes and captions align correctly. Additionally, full-bleed backgrounds are mandated to meet a minimum resolution, enhancing overall visual fidelity.

items.title: Launch of OCR-anchored Visual Send Backend in Platform Service 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces the `SEND_BACKEND=visual` feature for the WeWork service, enabling OCR-anchored, injection-free text sending. The implementation utilizes RapidOCR to identify conversation names and send buttons, ensuring a seamless user experience without direct memory access. Additionally, new tests have been added to validate the functionality, maintaining a focus on safety and compliance.

items.title: Streamlined CSV Export to Disk for Better Performance in Frontend Web 🚀
items.summary: This update enhances the CSV export functionality by streaming the file directly to disk instead of buffering it in memory. The new implementation reduces memory usage and improves user experience, especially for large exports. Additionally, error handling has been improved with localized messages for export failures across multiple languages.

items.title: Eliminate Dead-Storage WAV Mirror in Platform Service 🚀
items.summary: This update removes the fire-and-forget dead-storage WAV mirror to COS from the suno workers in openaihk and ttapi. The change prevents unbounded COS growth by discarding unreachable mirrored URLs while maintaining client behavior through the upstream WAV URL. All normal upload and transfer flows remain intact, ensuring seamless functionality.

items.title: Add auto-refresh toggle to usage history table in Frontend Web 🚀
items.summary: The usage history table now features an auto-refresh toggle that defaults to ON, allowing users to see new records every 15 seconds without manual refresh. This enhancement respects active filters and pagination, ensuring a seamless experience. The implementation includes internationalization support and optimizations to prevent data race conditions.

items.title: Introducing WeCom Automation Service in Platform Service 🚀
items.summary: We've launched a new automation service for WeCom (企业微信) within the PlatformService. This service mirrors the existing Wisdom appliance, providing capabilities like real-time message reading, QR login, and a robust API. Key improvements include a dedicated WeCom automation layer and enhanced security measures for data handling.

items.title: Enhance self-heal script for medium skill in Skills 🚀
items.summary: This update applies a self-healing resolver to the medium skill, ensuring that the script path correctly resolves even when `$SKILL_DIR` points to another loaded skill. The fix includes a per-block resolver and quoted usages, aligning with the patterns established in other skills. Validation confirms that the changes maintain functionality while improving error handling.

items.title: Enhance Self-Heal for Skills in Skills 🚀
items.summary: This update implements a self-healing script path for 16 skills, addressing issues caused by the `$SKILL_DIR` environment variable being clobbered. Each skill now anchors its script path, ensuring that it correctly resolves even when multiple skills are loaded simultaneously. The changes enhance reliability and prevent errors during execution.

items.title: New WeCom Automation Appliance Implementation Plan in Index 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces a comprehensive implementation plan for the WeCom automation appliance, building on the existing Wisdom framework. Key additions include a detailed how-to guide and several proof-of-concept scripts to analyze and capture WeCom data. The plan addresses the unique challenges of automating WeCom, emphasizing a hybrid approach that combines official APIs and UI automation techniques.

items.title: Introducing Xiaohongshu DM Auto-Reply Skill in Skills 🚀
items.summary: We've added a new skill for automatic reading and replying to Xiaohongshu private messages, specifically for Android devices using Nexior. This skill integrates seamlessly with the existing Computer Use tools, allowing users to manage their messages with a dry-run confirmation process to ensure safety and compliance. Key features include strict daily limits and a focus on user consent before sending any replies.

items.title: Streamlined Debug Logging for Gateway in Gateway 🚀
items.summary: This update significantly reduces the volume of debug logs generated by the gateway during API requests. Key changes include the removal of detailed logging for billing rule evaluations and task polling, while retaining essential error and warning logs. This enhancement aims to improve performance and reduce noise in log monitoring without compromising the ability to troubleshoot issues.

items.title: Enhance AKS Teardown to Purge TLS Secrets in Backend 🚀
items.summary: This update improves the AKS teardown process by ensuring that cert-manager TLS Secrets and Certificates are properly deleted in the correct order. The previous implementation left orphaned TLS Secrets, which could be resurrected by existing Certificates. The new logic explicitly deletes Certificates before removing the TLS Secrets, preventing any residual artifacts after tenant teardown.

items.title: Streamlined CSV Exports for Heavy Accounts in Backend 🚀
items.summary: This update replaces the manual keyset loop with a server-side cursor for CSV exports, significantly improving performance for large datasets. The new implementation allows for complete streaming of data, reducing export times from minutes to seconds. Additionally, error handling has been enhanced to ensure users receive feedback on any issues during the export process.

items.title: Revamped Usage Analytics with Dynamic Views in Frontend Web 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces a complete overhaul of the usage analytics page, featuring a new card layout with three dynamic views: Trend, Distribution, and Breakdown. The implementation of a dynamic Top-N feature enhances data visibility by ensuring significant APIs are always displayed, while minor ones are grouped under 'Others'. Additionally, internationalization support has been expanded to include multiple languages.

items.title: Integrate golden-v13 insights into AKS deployment runbook in Index 🚀
items.summary: This update merges key learnings from the golden-v13 launch into the Wisdom AKS deployment runbook. Significant changes include updates to the deployment manual, resource lifecycle management, and fixes for the EVAL-Windows hourly shutdown issue. The documentation now serves as a single source of truth, streamlining processes for new tenants and existing deployments.

items.title: Fix deliver stage firing issue for mid-compose uploads in Platform Service 🚀
items.summary: This update addresses a bug where the 'deliver' stage was incorrectly triggered during mid-compose asset uploads, causing the pipeline to remain stuck at 93%. The logic in the `_infer_stage` function has been refined to ensure that 'deliver' only fires when a finished video or the result manifest is uploaded. A regression test has been added to confirm that mid-compose uploads do not affect the delivery stage.

items.title: Streamline Skill Management with In-Repo Vendoring in Platform Service 🚀
items.summary: This update refactors the maestro worker by vendoring all agent skills directly in the repository, eliminating the need for build-time assembly from external sources. Key changes include the addition of 32 new vendored skills, improved reproducibility, and enhanced customizability for workflow skills. The Dockerfile has been simplified to copy the skills directly, ensuring a more stable and maintainable build process.

items.title: Fix skill-loader path issue for Zhihu in Skills 🚀
items.summary: This update resolves a critical bug where the `$SKILL_DIR` environment variable pointed to the last loaded skill, causing failures in script execution for the Zhihu skill. Key changes include modifying the Bash blocks in `SKILL.md` to independently resolve script paths and updating `blog.py` and `search.py` to eliminate reliance on `$SKILL_DIR`. The fix enhances reliability and ensures that the skill functions correctly even when multiple skills are loaded simultaneously.

items.title: Fix CronJob Pod Labeling to Eliminate 502 Errors in Backend 🚀
items.summary: This update relabels CronJob pods in the AuthBackend service to prevent them from being incorrectly selected by the service, which was causing intermittent 502 Bad Gateway errors. The change ensures that only web pods are included in the service endpoints, eliminating the connection issues without affecting the current deployment. All modifications are safe and will take effect the next time each CronJob runs.

items.title: Enhance Scheduled Tasks with Skill Binding Prompts in Nexior 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces a new flow for handling unbound skills in scheduled tasks. Users will now receive a prompt to bind skills that are not active, along with an option to save the task anyway using a 'force' flag. This change improves user experience by providing clearer guidance and reducing errors during task creation.

items.title: Enhance Error Handling with Skill Not Active Code in Platform Service 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces a new `SkillNotActiveError` to provide clearer error messages when a skill is not active for a user. Additionally, a `force` option has been added to allow users to bypass the active check when creating or updating skills, ensuring smoother user experience. The changes include updates to error handling and new tests to validate the functionality.

items.title: Revamp Maestro Step-Checklist UI in Nexior 🎨
items.summary: This update refines the maestro step-checklist by removing the thick connector line and introducing smaller, consistent icons. The new design features a clean list format with primary-color indicators for completed, active, and pending steps, enhancing the overall user experience. The logic remains unchanged, ensuring seamless integration with existing functionality.

items.title: Enhance Tenant Resource Management in Backend 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces significant improvements to tenant resource management by ensuring complete teardown of resources, including cert-manager TLS leftovers. It blocks bare DELETE requests that could orphan resources, enforcing a structured removal process. Additionally, the customer documentation has been rewritten to reflect the current Azure architecture and clarify resource lifecycle and billing.

items.title: Streamline HyperFrames Skills Integration in Index 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces the vendoring of approximately 12 routed HyperFrames skills into the maestro worker, eliminating the need for the previous Dockerfile git-clone-all method and associated patches. Key changes include retiring outdated mechanisms and addressing version drift between renderer and skills. The approach is legally sound under Apache-2.0, ensuring a more efficient workflow moving forward.

items.title: Revamp In-Progress Step-Checklist UI in Nexior 🎨
items.summary: This update enhances the in-progress step-checklist UI by introducing a bordered card design, aligning it with the success and failure states for a more professional look. Additionally, the custom flex list has been replaced with a native vertical stepper from Element Plus, providing a clearer visual timeline of progress. These changes improve consistency and accessibility without altering the underlying progress logic.

items.title: Ensure Real Subjects Route Correctly in Platform Service 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces a guardrail to ensure that real, named subjects are routed to the `/general-video` workflow instead of the `faceless-explainer`. This change prevents the loss of real imagery and improves processing speed. The documentation has been updated to clarify routing rules without altering any code paths.

items.title: Upgrade AKS Appliance to golden-v13 and Optimize RAM in Backend 🚀
items.summary: This update points the AKS appliance services to the newly activated golden-v13 image, resolving the previous hourly shutdown issue caused by evaluation-based images. Additionally, the VM RAM has been trimmed from 3G to 2G to better fit the LTSC and WeChat requirements, while maintaining the Kubernetes memory preset. The changes ensure a smoother operation and improved stability for tenant scenarios.

items.title: Revamp Enterprise Activation & Self-Heal in Wisdom 🚀
items.summary: This update resolves the WeChat-Bot appliance's hourly shutdown issue by replacing the evaluation Windows ISO with a genuine Enterprise LTSC version. Key enhancements include offline permanent activation using Microsoft Activation Scripts and a self-heal mechanism for the SMB share during first login. Additionally, the default RAM allocation for tenants has been optimized to 2GB, ensuring a smoother experience.

items.title: Enhance UI with Deterministic Pipeline Stages in Platform Service 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces six canonical, ordered pipeline stages for the Nexior maestro UI, improving the visibility of task progress. The stages are inferred from agent tool calls, ensuring a reliable step-checklist is displayed even when the agent does not provide detailed progress updates. Additionally, new tests have been added to validate the functionality, ensuring a robust implementation.

items.title: Enhance Video Task Progress with Step-Checklist in Nexior 🎉
items.summary: This update introduces a friendly step-checklist for in-progress video tasks in the maestro component, replacing the previous opaque progress line. The checklist displays six canonical pipeline stages, providing users with clear visibility into the task's progress. Additionally, it ensures backward compatibility for legacy tasks while enhancing accessibility features.

items.title: Automating Xiaohongshu: Feasibility and Cost Analysis in Index 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces a new feasibility plan for automating Xiaohongshu, detailing technical viability, cost estimates, and PoC strategies. Key findings indicate that direct UI automation on real devices is necessary for messaging, with domestic cloud phones emerging as the leading candidate for runtime. The document also addresses various risks and adjustments made during the review process.

items.title: Sync Check: Claude-Code and Codex Aligned in Clis 🚀
items.summary: This update confirms that the `claude-code` and `codex` services are already in sync with the latest documentation. No changes to the CLI are necessary as all relevant endpoints are fully implemented and match the model specifications. The findings indicate that the recent updates were guide-only, with no new API definitions required.

items.title: Streamline WeChat Console with Generic Proxy in Frontend Web 🚀
items.summary: This update refactors the WeChat appliance console to utilize a generic backend proxy, enhancing stability and reusability. The new `applianceRequest` method simplifies interactions, while client-side orchestration of the login QR improves performance and user experience. Minor security risks have been addressed, ensuring a clean and efficient implementation.

items.title: Streamlined Reverse Proxy for WeChat Console in Backend 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces a generic, owner-only reverse proxy for the WeChat console, enabling seamless API access for Wisdom/WeChat-bot appliances without browser restrictions. The new endpoint simplifies the architecture by replacing the previous two-endpoint design, allowing for future feature additions without backend changes. Comprehensive security measures are in place to prevent SSRF vulnerabilities, ensuring safe interactions with appliance APIs.

items.title: Enhance CC Switch GUI Guides for Claude Code and Codex in Backend 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces two new tutorials in Chinese for using CC Switch with Claude Code and Codex, eliminating the need for manual configuration edits. The guides provide step-by-step instructions and include live registration of the documents in the system. Additionally, existing parent guides have been updated to include links to these new resources, enhancing user accessibility and experience.

items.title: Optimize First Render by Vendor GSAP in Platform Service 🚀
items.summary: This update addresses the wasted first render issue in maestro jobs by ensuring GSAP is correctly placed in the render root before the initial render. A new callout in the SKILL.md contract guides users to copy GSAP into the render directory, preventing the common 404 error that leads to blank scenes. This change significantly reduces render time and enhances overall efficiency.

items.title: Enhance Connector Prompts for Clarity in Backend 🚀
items.summary: This update modifies the connector prompts to explicitly name the tool in each 'Try It' suggestion, addressing user feedback about ambiguity. Key changes include the addition of tool names to prompts across various connectors, ensuring users trigger the correct tool. The modifications enhance user experience by providing clearer instructions and maintaining accurate capabilities for each connector.

items.title: Enhance Volume Pricing Visibility in Frontend Web ⚡
items.summary: This update introduces a vibrant callout for volume pricing on the models and service pricing pages, making discounts more noticeable. The top-up tier selector now auto-rotates, showcasing the best pricing options dynamically. Additionally, localization improvements ensure clarity across all supported languages, enhancing user experience globally.

items.title: Enhance Documentation Search with Command Palette in Frontend Web 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces a site-wide documentation search command palette to PlatformFrontend, utilizing the existing public search API. Key features include keyboard navigation, responsive design, and locale-aware search parameters, enhancing user experience by allowing quick access to documentation from anywhere in the app.

items.title: Expanding Wisdom's Automation Goals in Index 🚀
items.summary: A new plan document has been added to outline the research on expanding the Wisdom platform's automation capabilities across various messaging platforms. Key findings indicate immediate opportunities with WeChat and the establishment of an Android fleet for Douyin and Xiaohongshu. The document also highlights legal considerations and operational costs associated with different platforms, ensuring a strategic approach to automation.

items.title: Enhance Connector Details for Unconnected Users in Frontend Web 🚀
items.summary: This update improves the connectors page by providing detailed information for unconnected connectors. Users can now see a full description and a locked 'Try It' section, enhancing clarity on connector capabilities before making a connection. Additionally, a friendly hint is displayed under Tool Permissions, guiding users on how to access tools after connecting.

items.title: Boost frame capture speed with chrome-headless-shell in Platform Service 🚀
items.summary: This update integrates chrome-headless-shell into the maestro worker image, significantly enhancing frame capture performance. The new capture path reduces frame capture time from 87.3 seconds to just 41.1 seconds, achieving nearly 2x faster rendering. This improvement is applicable across various environments, including those without GPU support.

items.title: Enhance routing for llms-full.txt in Frontend Web 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces routing for `/llms-full.txt` and `/.well-known/llms-full.txt` through the frontend to PlatformBackend, ensuring that AI crawlers receive the correct documentation instead of HTML. Key modifications include new location blocks in `nginx.conf.template` and an updated entry in `worker/index.mjs` to handle the new paths. These changes mirror the existing structure for `/llms.txt`, maintaining consistency across the application.

items.title: Enhance LLM Integration with Full Documentation in Backend 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces `llms-full.txt`, a comprehensive resource that consolidates all public integration guides into a single document, making it easier for AI tools to access the necessary content. Additionally, it resolves a defect in the `robots.txt` file, ensuring proper indexing of the new resource. The changes improve efficiency by allowing a single fetch for all guides instead of multiple crawls.

items.title: Fix chat model playground rendering issue in Frontend Web 🚀
items.summary: This update resolves a case-sensitivity bug preventing the live-test playground from rendering on chat model detail pages. By normalizing the lookup key to lowercase, all seven chat providers are now correctly mapped, ensuring the playground is displayed as intended. The fix has been manually verified against live data, confirming the expected functionality.

items.title: Seamless WeChat QR Login and API Playground in Frontend Web 🚀
items.summary: This update enhances the WeChat-bot appliance console by integrating an embedded QR login feature, allowing users to authenticate without leaving the page. Additionally, a new API test playground is introduced for connected users, enabling them to interact with various endpoints directly. Other improvements include token masking, conditional power buttons, and professional code blocks for better user experience.

items.title: Enhance Async Task Polling with Backoff in Platform Service 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces a wall-clock backoff mechanism for asynchronous task polling in the aichat2 worker, addressing issues with premature task abandonment. By implementing a delay between consecutive polls based on task repetition, the system can now handle longer rendering times effectively. Key files modified include configuration settings and orchestrator logic to support this new feature.

items.title: Fix translation keys in llms.txt API titles in Backend 🚀
items.summary: This update resolves issues with raw translation placeholder keys appearing in the llms.txt file. The fix ensures that both API titles and introductions are processed correctly, preventing any raw `$t()` keys from being exposed. The changes enhance the clarity of the API documentation for crawlers and improve overall consistency in title resolution.

items.title: Fix landing behavior for documents with children in Frontend Web 🚀
items.summary: This update addresses an issue where the landing page incorrectly navigated to a parent document instead of the first child document when children were present. The logic has been refined to ensure that if a root document has children, the navigation will always descend to the first child. This change improves user experience by providing a more intuitive document structure.

items.title: Fix GSAP Loading for Scene Rendering in Platform Service 🎨
items.summary: This update addresses the issue of blank scenes in rendered videos by changing how GSAP is served. The Dockerfile now rewrites GSAP references to be served from the same origin, ensuring that compositions load correctly. Additionally, the image validation confirms that the fix resolves the rendering issues, leading to successful video outputs.

items.title: Update Node Types for Enhanced Compatibility in Frontend Web 🚀
items.summary: This update bumps the @types/node package from version 26.0.1 to 26.1.0, ensuring better compatibility and support for TypeScript features. The changes include modifications to both package.json and package-lock.json, reflecting the new version. This will help maintain the stability and performance of the project.
